title: 10 best herbal capsules for brain fog
intro_desc: Herbal capsules for brain fog are dietary supplements made from natural herbs that help alleviate mental clarity and focus. They aid in reducing mental fogginess, improving memory, and enhancing cognitive function. The best herbal capsules for brain fog include Bacopa monnieri, Ginkgo biloba, Panax ginseng, Rhodiola rosea, Centella asiatica, Withania somnifera, Schisandra chinensis, Vitex agnus-castus, Ocimum sanctum, and Panax quinquefolius. If left untreated, brain fog can significantly impact daily life, causing difficulty completing tasks, decreased productivity, and increased stress levels, ultimately affecting relationships, work performance, and overall well-being.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Brahmi capsules helps with brain fog because they contain extracts of Bacopa monnieri, a natural herb that has been used for centuries in traditional Ayurvedic medicine to enhance cognitive function and memory. The active compounds in brahmi, such as bacosides, have been shown to improve blood flow to the brain, increase neurotransmitter activity, and reduce oxidative stress, leading to enhanced focus, clarity, and mental acuity. By supporting brain health, brahmi capsules can help alleviate feelings of mental fogginess and promote a sense of mental clarity and sharpness.
remedy_properties: Cognitive Enhancing Compounds: Brahmi contains compounds like bacosides, saponins, and alkaloids that enhance cognitive function by improving memory, attention, and concentration.

Neuroprotective Properties: Brahmi has neuroprotective properties that help protect the brain from oxidative stress and inflammation, which can contribute to brain fog and other cognitive impairment symptoms.

Anxiolytic and Stress-Relieving Compounds: Brahmi contains anxiolytic (anxiety-reducing) compounds like flavonoids and glycosides that help reduce stress levels and promote relaxation, leading to improved mental clarity.
remedy_constituents: Bacosides: Bacosides are a group of glycosides responsible for the nootropic properties of Bacopa monnieri. They help reduce brain fog by improving memory, cognitive function, and neural communication.

Bacopasides: Bacopasides are another group of glycosides found in Bacopa monnieri, which may help protect against oxidative stress and inflammation in the brain. This may contribute to improved cognitive function and reduced brain fog.

Clerodane diterpenoids: Clerodane diterpenoids are a type of terpenoid found in Bacopa monnieri, which may have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects. These properties may help protect against age-related cognitive decline and improve memory and cognitive function.
remedy_parts: Leaves: The leaves of the Bacopa monnieri plant are primarily used due to their high concentration of bioactive compounds, including bacosides.
Roots: The roots are used as they are rich in alkaloids and saponins, which have been shown to improve memory and cognitive function.
Stems: The stems are occasionally used in combination with the leaves and roots, as they may contribute to the overall potency of the extract.
remedy_recipe: Weigh 250 grams of dried bacopa monnieri root and stems in a digital scale.
Grind the weighed bacopa monnieri into a fine powder using a coffee grinder for 10 minutes.
Mix 5 grams of silica gel with 250 grams of the powdered bacopa monnieri in a bowl.
Fill 100 capsules with the powder mixture and seal them using a capsule filling machine for 10 minutes.
Store the herbal bacopa monnieri capsules in a cool dry place for up to 2 years.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Maidenhair tree capsules helps with brain fog because they contain a unique combination of bioactive compounds, including flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenes. These substances work together to enhance blood flow to the brain, improving cognitive function and reducing mental fatigue. Additionally, maidenhair tree's natural antioxidants help protect the brain from oxidative stress, promoting healthy neuronal communication and reducing feelings of mental haze. By supporting overall brain health, maidenhair tree capsules can help individuals regain clarity and focus.
remedy_properties: Flavonoids: These antioxidants help improve blood circulation to the brain, reducing oxygen deprivation and increasing cognitive function. This leads to improved focus, concentration, and memory.
Terpenes: These compounds have anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce oxidative stress and inflammation in the brain, which can contribute to brain fog and other cognitive impairments.
Ginkgolides: These unique compounds are believed to act as a "brain-awakening" agent, improving blood flow and oxygen delivery to the brain, leading to enhanced mental clarity and reduced fatigue.
remedy_constituents: Bilobalide: This terpene has antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, which help reduce oxidative stress and inflammation in the brain, thus alleviating brain fog.

Flavonoids (including quercetin and kaempferol): These phenolic compounds have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, which can improve blood flow to the brain, reduce oxidative stress, and enhance cognitive function.

Ginkgolides (including ginkgolide A and B): These diterpene lactones have anti-inflammatory properties and can inhibit platelet-activating factor (PAF), a compound that can cause blood vessels to constrict and reduce blood flow to the brain, thus alleviating brain fog.
remedy_parts: Leaves: The leaves are the most commonly used part of Ginkgo biloba due to their high concentration of flavonoids and terpenoids, which are believed to improve cognitive function.
Seeds: The seeds are also widely used due to their high content of ginkgolides, which are known to improve blood flow to the brain and reduce symptoms of brain fog.
Roots: The roots are occasionally used as a source of ginkgolides and flavonoids, although they are less commonly used than the leaves and seeds.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 250-500 grams of ginkgo biloba leaves from mature trees in the fall season.
Dry the leaves in a low-temperature oven at 50-60 degrees celsius for 2-3 hours.
Grind the dried ginkgo biloba leaves into a fine powder using a mill or blender.
Mix 500mg of the ginkgo biloba powder with 100mg of filler material like cellulose in a capsule.
Fill 100-200 capsules with the ginkgo biloba mixture using an automatic encapsulator.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Golden root capsules helps with brain fog because they contain Rhodiola rosea, an adaptogenic herb that enhances mental clarity and focus. The active compounds in golden root capsules, such as salidroside and rosin, have been shown to improve cognitive function by increasing oxygenation of the brain, boosting neurotransmitters like serotonin and dopamine, and reducing inflammation that can impair mental performance. As a result, taking golden root capsules regularly may help alleviate feelings of mental fogginess, improving overall mental acuity and productivity.
remedy_properties: Adaptogenic effects: Golden root capsules have adaptogenic properties, which means they help your body adapt to stress and improve its ability to cope with mental fatigue, leading to reduced brain fog.
Antioxidant properties: Golden root contains antioxidants that help protect the brain from oxidative stress and inflammation, which can contribute to brain fog and impaired cognitive function.

Cognitive-boosting compounds: Golden root capsules contain compounds like sesquiterpenoids and saponins, which have been shown to improve memory, focus, and mental clarity, helping to alleviate symptoms of brain fog.
remedy_constituents: Rosavin: It helps alleviate brain fog by reducing stress and anxiety, allowing the brain to function more efficiently.
Salidroside: This compound has antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, which can help protect brain cells from damage and improve cognitive function.
Tyrosol: Tyrosol acts as a mood stabilizer and neuroprotectant, helping to reduce oxidative stress and inflammation in the brain, leading to improved mental clarity and focus.
remedy_parts: Roots: Roots are the primary source of Rhodiola rosea's bioactive compounds, which are responsible for its adaptogenic and cognitive-enhancing effects.
Leaves: Leaves contain a significant amount of rosmarinic acid, a compound that may help improve mental performance and reduce brain fog.
Rhyzomes: Rhyzomes are known to contain a high concentration of rhodiolosides, which are believed to contribute to Rhodiola rosea's cognitive-enhancing properties and ability to reduce fatigue.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 500 grams of dried rhodiola rosea roots with 1.5% to 2% rosavin content.
Grind the roots into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle for 10 minutes.
Mix 250 grams of the powdered roots with 250 grams of gelatin in a bowl.
Fill a capsule machine with the gelatin-root mixture and seal 100 capsules for 10 minutes.
Store the capsules in an airtight container in a cool dark place for 6 months.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: St John's Wort capsules helps with brain fog because they contain hyperforin, a natural compound that enhances neurotransmitter activity in the brain. This boost can help improve mental clarity and focus by increasing the release of serotonin, dopamine, and norepinephrine, which are essential for attention, memory, and mood regulation. Additionally, St John's Wort has been shown to have anti-inflammatory properties that may help reduce oxidative stress and inflammation in the brain, leading to improved cognitive function and a sense of mental clarity.
remedy_properties: Hyperforin: This compound has been shown to increase serotonin levels in the brain, which can help improve mood and reduce feelings of fogginess and mental clarity.

Flavonoids and phenolic acids: These plant compounds have antioxidant properties, which can help protect against oxidative stress and inflammation in the brain, potentially contributing to improved cognitive function and reduced brain fog.

Adhyperforin: This compound has been shown to have a potent ability to inhibit the breakdown of neurotransmitters such as serotonin and dopamine, which are essential for maintaining clear thinking and focus.
remedy_constituents: Hyperforin: This phenolic compound has neuroprotective properties, which may help improve cognitive function and reduce brain fog by preventing oxidative stress and inflammation in the brain.

Hypericin: This naphthodianthone compound has antioxidant and anti-inflammatory effects, which may help alleviate brain fog by reducing oxidative damage and inflammation in the brain and promoting healthy neurotransmitter function.

Flavonoids (e.g., rutin, quercetin): These plant-based compounds have antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, which may help improve cognitive function and reduce brain fog by protecting neurons from oxidative stress and inflammation, promoting healthy blood flow to the brain, and supporting the health of blood-brain barrier.
remedy_parts: Leaves: The most used part of Hypericum perforatum for brain fog is Leaves, as they contain flavonoids and xanthones, which have been shown to improve cognitive function and reduce memory loss.
Flowers: The flowers are also commonly used due to their high content of hypericin and hyperforin, which have neuroprotective properties and can help alleviate brain fog symptoms.
Seeds: Seeds are another part used in Hypericum perforatum supplements for brain fog, as they contain flavonoids and other compounds that may help improve cognitive function and memory.
remedy_recipe: Harvest hypericum perforatum flowers and leaves at peak potency in mid to late summer for optimal use.
Dry the harvested hypericum perforatum flowers and leaves in a single layer at 35 degrees celsius for 2 hours.
Crush 2 parts of the dried hypericum perforatum flowers and 1 part of the dried leaves into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle.
Mix 300 milligrams of the powder with 50 milligrams of gelatin and 50 milligrams of purified water to create a capsule filling.
Fill empty gelatin capsules with the mixture from step 4 using a capsule filling machine or a dropper.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Ashwagandha capsules helps with brain fog because of its ability to reduce stress and anxiety levels, which are often contributing factors to mental fogginess. The herb's adaptogenic properties allow it to help the body cope with cortisol spikes, promoting a sense of calm and clarity. Additionally, ashwagandha is known to improve cognitive function by increasing neurotransmitter production, enhancing memory and focus, and even reducing inflammation that can impede brain function. This holistic approach helps to clear mental fog, allowing individuals to think more clearly and stay focused throughout the day.
remedy_properties: Adaptogenic Properties: Ashwagandha's adaptogens help regulate stress hormones, such as cortisol, to reduce mental fatigue and improve focus, leading to reduced brain fog.

Antioxidant Activity: Ashwagandha's antioxidants neutralize free radicals that damage brain cells, promoting healthy neuronal function and reducing oxidative stress, which can contribute to brain fog.

Neuroprotective Properties: Ashwagandha has been shown to increase the production of a neurotransmitter called GABA, which helps regulate sleep, relaxation, and focus, thereby reducing brain fog and improving cognitive clarity.
remedy_constituents: Withanolides: These steroidal lactones have potent antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, which help reduce oxidative stress and inflammation in the brain, thereby alleviating brain fog.

Alkaloids (Withanine, Withanone): These compounds have neuroprotective and nootropic effects, which help improve cognitive function, enhance memory, and reduce the risk of age-related cognitive decline associated with brain fog.

Flavonoids (Withaflavone, Withastranol): These polyphenolic compounds have antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, which help protect the brain from oxidative stress and inflammation, promoting improved cognitive function and reduced brain fog.
remedy_parts: Roots: Containing bioactive compounds like withanolides, which are believed to have neuroprotective and nootropic effects.
Leaves: Rich in antioxidants and other phytochemicals that help in improving cognitive function and reducing oxidative stress.
Seeds: Containing withanolides and other nutrients that support brain health and may help alleviate symptoms of brain fog.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 50 grams of dried withania somnifera roots from a reputable supplier and store them in an airtight container.
Weigh 10 grams of the dried withania somnifera roots and grind them into a fine powder using a coffee grinder.
Sift the ground powder through a 60-mesh sieve to remove any lumps or large particles.
Fill 50 empty gelatin capsules with approximately 0.25 grams of the sifted powder each and seal the capsules.
Label and date the capsules and store them in a cool dark place for up to 2 years.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Ginseng capsules helps with brain fog because they contain adaptogenic compounds that help increase blood flow to the brain, boosting cognitive function and clarity. The herb's ability to balance cortisol levels also contributes to its benefits, as high cortisol can cause mental fogginess. Additionally, ginseng's antioxidant properties protect the brain from oxidative stress, which can impair cognitive performance. By promoting a healthy brain environment, herbal ginseng capsules can help alleviate symptoms of brain fog and improve overall mental acuity.
remedy_properties: Improves Cerebral Blood Flow: Ginseng capsules increase blood flow to the brain, which helps to clear out toxic substances and improve cognitive function, reducing feelings of brain fog.
Enhances Neuroprotection: Ginseng's antioxidant properties help protect the brain from damage caused by free radicals, which can contribute to cognitive decline and foggy thinking.

Boosts Neurotransmitter Function: Ginseng capsules contain compounds that stimulate the production of neurotransmitters like serotonin, dopamine, and acetylcholine, which are essential for focus, attention, and mental clarity.
remedy_constituents: Ginsenosides: These triterpenoid saponins help alleviate brain fog by improving blood flow to the brain, enhancing cognitive function, and reducing oxidative stress.

Eleutherosides: These triterpenoid saponins have been found to improve mental clarity and reduce fatigue by enhancing the body's adaptogenic response to stress, thereby supporting overall cognitive function.

Panaxynol: This triterpenoid saponin has been shown to have neuroprotective properties, helping to prevent the degradation of neurotransmitters and improve cognitive function, thus reducing brain fog.
remedy_parts: Roots: They contain the highest concentration of ginsenosides, which are believed to have neuroprotective effects and improve cognitive function.
Rhyzomes: Similar to roots, rhyzomes also contain ginsenosides, making them a popular choice for supplements aimed at improving brain function.

Barks: Barks of Panax ginseng are sometimes used in traditional medicine, although they may not be as widely used as roots or rhyzomes, they still contain some bioactive compounds that may help with brain fog.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh panax ginseng roots after 5-10 years of growth in well-drained soil and partial shade conditions.
Dry the harvested roots in a temperature-controlled environment at 40-50 degrees celsius for 3-5 days.
Grind the dried roots into a fine powder using a grinder with a 40-mesh sieve size.
Mix 400 mg of the ground panax ginseng powder with 100 mg of cellulose and 50 mg of magnesium stearate.
Fill each empty gelatin capsule with the powder mixture and seal using a manual or automatic capsule filler.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Sage capsules helps with brain fog because they contain the herb Salvia officinalis, which has been traditionally used to improve cognitive function and memory. The active compounds in sage, such as rosmarinic acid and carnosic acid, have been shown to increase blood flow to the brain and enhance neural communication. This can help alleviate symptoms of brain fog, including difficulty concentrating, forgetfulness, and mental fogginess, allowing for improved focus and mental clarity.
remedy_properties: Cognitive Enhancing Compounds: Sage contains compounds like rosmarinic acid, carnosic acid, and ursolic acid which have been shown to improve cognitive function by increasing blood flow to the brain, enhancing memory, and improving mental clarity.

Anti-Inflammatory Effects: The antioxidants and polyphenols in sage capsules have potent anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce oxidative stress and inflammation in the brain, which are often linked to brain fog and cognitive decline.

Neuroprotective Compounds: Sage contains compounds like caffeic acid and ellagic acid which have been shown to have neuroprotective effects, potentially helping to protect against age-related cognitive decline and reducing the risk of neurological diseases such as Alzheimer's.
remedy_constituents: Rosmarinic acid: A phenolic compound that helps reduce inflammation in the brain, thereby alleviating symptoms of brain fog and improving cognitive function.

Carnosic acid: A phenolic diterpene that has neuroprotective properties, antioxidant effects, and anti-inflammatory actions, which collectively contribute to improved mental clarity and reduced brain fog.

Salvianolic acids: A group of phenolic acids that possess antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, helping to protect the brain from oxidative stress and inflammation, and thereby reducing the symptoms of brain fog.
remedy_parts: Leaves: They are used due to their high concentration of salvinic acids, which have been shown to improve cognitive function and reduce mental fatigue.
Flowers: They are used to make capsules for brain fog due to their flavonoid content, which may have neuroprotective and memory-enhancing effects.
Seeds: They are used because they contain a higher concentration of essential oils, including thujone, which may help improve focus and mental clarity.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 1 part salvia officinalis leaves at the peak of potency when the plant is in full bloom.
Dry the harvested leaves at 30 degrees celsius for 2 hours to remove excess moisture.
Grind the dried leaves into a fine powder using a high-speed blender for 1 minute.
Mix 250 milligrams of the powder with 1000 milligrams of vegetable cellulose in a bowl.
Fill the powder mixture into 0 00 empty vegetarian capsules using a capsule filler machine.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Asiatic pennywort capsules helps with brain fog because they contain a natural compound called centella asiatica, which has been shown to improve blood flow to the brain and increase oxygenation. This can help to reduce symptoms of fatigue, confusion, and mental fogginess by providing essential nutrients and energy to the brain cells. Additionally, Asiatic pennywort capsules have anti-inflammatory properties that may help to soothe and protect the brain from damage caused by oxidative stress, further supporting cognitive function and clarity.
remedy_properties: Antioxidant properties: Asiatic Pennywort contains antioxidants that help protect the brain from oxidative stress and inflammation, which can contribute to brain fog.
Vasodilatory properties: The herb's vasodilatory effects help improve blood flow to the brain, increasing oxygenation and reducing feelings of mental fogginess.

Neuroprotective properties: Asiatic Pennywort contains neuroprotective compounds that may help prevent or slow down the progression of neurodegenerative diseases such as Alzheimer's and Parkinson's, which can contribute to brain fog.
remedy_constituents: Triterpenoids: These compounds, particularly asiaticoside and madecassoside, help with brain fog by improving blood flow to the brain, enhancing cognitive function, and reducing inflammation that can lead to mental fatigue.

Phenolic acids: Phenolic acids like caffeic acid and ferulic acid in Centella asiatica capsules have antioxidant properties, which help protect the brain from oxidative stress and inflammation, thereby reducing symptoms of brain fog and improving mental clarity.

Asiatic acid: Asiatic acid, a triterpenoid saponin, has been shown to improve memory and cognitive function by modulating neurotransmitter activity, reducing inflammation, and promoting neuroprotection, all of which can help alleviate brain fog and improve mental performance.
remedy_parts: Leaves: They are rich in active compounds like asiatic acid and madecassic acid, which have been shown to improve cognitive function and reduce brain fog.
Roots: The roots of Centella asiatica contain triterpenoid saponins, which have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that can help alleviate brain fog and improve mental clarity.
Barks: The barks of Centella asiatica contain flavonoids and phenolic acids, which have been found to have neuroprotective effects and may help reduce oxidative stress and improve brain function.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 30g of centella asiatica leaves and stems from a clean and pesticide-free environment.
Dry the harvested centella asiatica in a low-temperature oven at 40°c for 2 hours.
Grind the dried centella asiatica into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle.
Mix 500mg of the centella asiatica powder with 200mg of starch and 100mg of gelatin.
Fill the mixture into empty gelatin capsules in a clean and sanitary environment.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Maypop capsules helps with brain fog because they contain Passiflora incarnata, a natural herb that has been used for centuries to calm the mind and promote mental clarity. The active compounds in maypop, including flavonoids and alkaloids, work together to reduce anxiety and stress levels, which can contribute to brain fog. By supporting healthy brain function, maypop capsules may help improve focus, concentration, and memory, leaving you feeling more alert and mentally clear throughout the day.
remedy_properties: Antioxidant Properties: Maypop capsules contain antioxidants like flavonoids, alkaloids, and ascorbic acid that help protect the brain from oxidative stress and inflammation, which can contribute to cognitive impairment and brain fog.

Anxiolytic (Anxiety-Reducing) Effects: The herbal extract in Maypop capsules has been shown to possess anxiolytic properties, helping to reduce anxiety levels by binding to GABA receptors in the brain, which can help alleviate symptoms of brain fog such as mental fogginess and difficulty concentrating.

GABA-Mimetic Properties: Maypop contains compounds that mimic the effects of Gamma-Aminobutyric Acid (GABA), a neurotransmitter that helps regulate sleep, relaxation, and cognitive function. By enhancing GABA activity, Maypop capsules may help improve focus, memory, and overall brain clarity.
remedy_constituents: Flavonoids: They help alleviate brain fog by reducing inflammation and oxidative stress in the brain, which can contribute to cognitive impairment.

Alkaloids (specifically Harmine and Harmaline): They aid in improving focus and mental clarity by inhibiting the reuptake of neurotransmitters like serotonin and dopamine, promoting a sense of calm and relaxation.
Isoorientin: It has been shown to possess antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, which can help mitigate the negative effects of oxidative stress on the brain and improve cognitive function.
remedy_parts: Leaves: The leaves of Passiflora incarnata are used due to their high concentration of flavonoids, such as kaempferol and quercetin, which have neuroprotective properties.

Fruits: The fruits of Passiflora incarnata are used because they contain a higher amount of passifloric acid, a flavonoid that has been traditionally used to alleviate anxiety and improve cognitive function.

Roots: The roots of Passiflora incarnata are used as they contain a higher concentration of alkaloids, such as passiflorine, which have sedative and anti-anxiety effects that can help alleviate brain fog.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 200g of fresh passiflora incarnata flowers in the morning to ensure optimal potency.
Dry the harvested flowers in a low-temperature dehydrator at 40°c for 24 hours to prevent degradation.
Grind 100g of dried flowers into a fine powder using a coffee grinder to increase surface area.
Mix 20g of the powdered flowers with 80g of vegetable cellulose powder in a bowl to create a uniform blend.
Fill 100 empty vegetarian capsules with the powder blend using a capsule filler and seal tightly.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Lemon balm capsules helps with brain fog because they contain Melissa officinalis, a natural herb that has been used for centuries to promote cognitive function and mental clarity. The active compounds in lemon balm, such as rosmarinic acid and citral, work to calm the mind and reduce feelings of mental fogginess. By doing so, lemon balm capsules help to improve concentration, memory, and overall brain function, leaving individuals feeling more focused and mentally sharp.
remedy_properties: Adaptogenic effects: Lemon balm capsules help reduce stress and anxiety, which are common causes of brain fog, by modulating cortisol levels and promoting relaxation.

Antioxidant activity: Lemon balm contains antioxidants that neutralize free radicals in the brain, reducing oxidative stress and inflammation that can contribute to cognitive impairment and brain fog.

Cholinergic activity: Lemon balm has been shown to increase acetylcholine levels in the brain, a neurotransmitter involved in attention, memory, and cognitive processing, helping to improve mental clarity and reduce brain fog.
remedy_constituents: Rosmarinic acid: A phenolic compound that helps reduce oxidative stress and inflammation in the brain, contributing to improved mental clarity and reduced brain fog.
Linalool: A terpene that acts as a GABA receptor agonist, promoting relaxation and reducing anxiety, which can contribute to brain fog, making it easier to focus and concentrate.
Limonene: A terpene that has been shown to have neuroprotective effects, helping to reduce inflammation and oxidative stress in the brain, and improve memory and cognitive function.
remedy_parts: Leaves: The leaves are the most commonly used part, as they contain the highest concentration of melissic acid and other compounds that are believed to promote relaxation and improve cognitive function.
Flowers: The flowers are also used due to their high content of essential oils, which have a calming effect on the mind and body, helping to alleviate brain fog.
Stems: The stems are used in some cases, as they contain a smaller amount of essential oils and melissic acid, which can still contribute to the overall benefits of the capsules for brain fog.
remedy_recipe: Harvest melissa officinalis leaves and flowers in the morning after the dew has evaporated for optimal freshness.
Dry the harvested melissa officinalis leaves and flowers in a low-temperature oven at 30 degrees celsius for 2 hours.
Grind the dried melissa officinalis leaves and flowers into a fine powder using a coffee grinder.
Mix the powdered melissa officinalis with a binder such as gelatin and a small amount of water to create a uniform mixture.
Fill gelatin capsules with the uniform mixture of powdered melissa officinalis and seal immediately to preserve potency.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Tea capsules helps with brain fog because they contain potent herbs that stimulate blood flow to the brain, increasing oxygenation and nutrients. The capsules typically blend adaptogenic herbs like ashwagandha, Bacopa monnieri, and Ginkgo biloba, which work together to enhance focus, clarity, and mental acuity. By promoting a healthy cognitive environment, these herbal tea capsules can help alleviate feelings of mental fogginess, allowing individuals to think more clearly and stay mentally sharp throughout the day.
remedy_properties: Antioxidants: Herbal teas rich in antioxidants, such as green tea or ginkgo biloba, help protect the brain from oxidative stress and inflammation, which can contribute to brain fog.

Cognitive-enhancing compounds: Herbs like Bacopa monnieri, Ginkgo biloba, and Ashwagandha contain compounds that have been shown to improve memory, attention, and mental clarity by enhancing neurotransmitter activity and blood flow to the brain.

Adaptogenic properties: Adaptogenic herbs like Ashwagandha, Rhodiola, and Ginseng help the body adapt to stress and promote a sense of calm and focus, which can help alleviate brain fog caused by stress and anxiety.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in antioxidants and adaptogenic compounds, tea roots help reduce oxidative stress and promote mental clarity.
Barks: Containing a high concentration of caffeine and other xanthines, tea barks can improve focus and attention by stimulating the central nervous system.
remedy_recipe: Harvest high-quality dried green tea leaves camellia sinensis that are free from contaminants and additives.
Grind the dried green tea leaves into a fine powder using a spice grinder or mortar and pestle for approximately 30 seconds.
Mix the ground green tea powder with 250-500mg of other herbal ingredients such as bacopa monnieri and ginkgo biloba in a small bowl for 2 minutes.
Fill empty capsules with the herbal blend using an encapsulating machine or a spoon for a total weight of 500mg per capsule.
Store the filled capsules in a cool dark place for up to 6 months or longer by refrigerating them at a temperature below 40 degrees fahrenheit.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Peppermint capsules helps with brain fog because they increase oxygen flow to the brain, allowing for clearer thinking and improved mental clarity. The menthol content in peppermint also enhances blood circulation, which can help alleviate feelings of fatigue and mental fogginess. Additionally, peppermint's calming effects on the digestive system can reduce stress and anxiety, common contributors to brain fog, making it easier to stay focused and alert throughout the day.
remedy_properties: Menthol content: Menthol, a key component of peppermint oil, has been shown to increase blood flow to the brain, helping to reduce feelings of mental fogginess and improve focus.
Antioxidant activity: Peppermint's high antioxidant content helps to protect the brain from oxidative stress and inflammation, which can contribute to cognitive impairment and brain fog.

Gastrointestinal benefits: Peppermint capsules have been shown to soothe digestive issues such as irritable bowel syndrome (IBS), which is often associated with brain fog. By alleviating these symptoms, peppermint may also help improve mental clarity.
remedy_parts: Roots: Peppermint roots contain a high concentration of menthol, which has been shown to improve mental clarity and focus, making it an effective ingredient in brain fog supplements.

Buds: Peppermint buds have a higher menthone content than other parts of the plant, which is responsible for its analgesic and anti-inflammatory properties, making them beneficial for reducing mental fogginess.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 20 grams of fresh peppermint leaves from healthy plants at dawn or early morning for optimal potency.
Rinse the harvested peppermint leaves with filtered water and pat dry with paper towels to remove excess moisture.
Combine the dried peppermint leaves with 100 milliliters of carrier oil such as coconut or olive oil in a clean glass jar and store at room temperature for four weeks.
After four weeks, strain the mixture through cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another clean glass jar, discarding the solids and retaining the infused oil.
Fill empty vegetarian capsules with 500 milligrams of the peppermint-infused oil and seal tightly, storing them in an airtight container at room temperature for up to six months.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Valerian capsules helps with brain fog because they promote a sense of clarity and focus by increasing the activity of neurotransmitters such as GABA, serotonin, and acetylcholine. These neurotransmitters play a crucial role in regulating attention, memory, and mental processing speed. By supporting the production and release of these neurotransmitters, valerian capsules can help to alleviate feelings of mental fogginess and improve cognitive function. Additionally, valerian root has been shown to reduce stress and anxiety, which are common underlying causes of brain fog.
remedy_properties: GABA-like effects: Valerian root has been shown to interact with GABA receptors in the brain, promoting relaxation and reducing anxiety, which can help alleviate symptoms of brain fog by calming an overactive mind.

Adaptogenic properties: Valerian is considered an adaptogen, meaning it helps the body adapt to stress and promotes balance in the nervous system, which can help reduce feelings of fatigue, mental fogginess, and decreased mental clarity that often accompany brain fog.

Antioxidant activity: Valerian root contains a range of antioxidants, including flavonoids and phenolic acids, which help protect the brain from oxidative stress and inflammation, which are thought to contribute to brain fog and other cognitive impairments.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in valepotriates, a group of compounds that have been shown to improve cognitive function and reduce anxiety.
Rhyzomes: Contains a high concentration of valerenic acid, which has been demonstrated to have sedative and anxiolytic effects.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh valeriana officinalis roots from a reputable supplier or your own garden for optimal potency.
Clean and dry the harvested roots to remove any impurities and excess moisture within 24 hours.
Chop the dried roots into small pieces and soak them in 190 degrees fahrenheit boiling water for 20 minutes.
Steep the mixture for an additional 30 minutes before filtering out the solids and reducing the liquid to a concentrated extract within one hour.
Fill empty gelatin capsules with 250 milligrams of the prepared valeriana officinalis extract and store them in an airtight container.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Turmeric capsules helps with brain fog because they contain curcumin, a powerful compound that crosses the blood-brain barrier to alleviate cognitive impairment. Curcumin has been shown to improve memory and cognitive function by reducing oxidative stress and inflammation in the brain. Additionally, it enhances blood flow to the brain, allowing for better oxygenation and nutrient delivery to neural cells. As a result, turmeric capsules can help clarify mental fogginess, improving focus, concentration, and mental clarity.
remedy_properties: Curcumin: Curcumin, a polyphenol compound found in turmeric, has potent anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that help reduce oxidative stress and inflammation in the brain, thereby alleviating symptoms of brain fog.

Bioavailability-enhancing Piperine: Turmeric capsules often contain piperine, a bioactive compound found in black pepper, which increases the absorption and bioavailability of curcumin in the body, allowing for greater potency in improving cognitive function and reducing brain fog.

Curcuminoids' Neuroprotective Effects: The curcuminoids present in turmeric capsules have been shown to possess neuroprotective properties that help protect against neuronal damage, promote neuroplasticity, and improve memory and cognitive function, all of which can contribute to reduced symptoms of brain fog.
remedy_parts: Rhyzomes: Rich in curcuminoids, particularly curcumin, which has potent anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that help alleviate symptoms of brain fog.
Roots: Contain a higher concentration of essential oils and phenolic compounds, including turmerone, which has been shown to have neuroprotective effects and improve cognitive function.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 500g of dried turmeric roots curcuma longa from a trusted supplier or local market.
Grind the dried turmeric roots into a fine powder using a spice grinder or mortar and pestle.
Mix 2000mg of the ground turmeric powder with 100mg of guggul resin and 50mg of bacopa monnieri extract in a bowl.
Fill empty gelatin capsules with approximately 500mg of the herbal mixture and seal tightly.
Store the finished curcuma longa capsules in an airtight container at room temperature for up to 6 months.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Milk thistle capsules helps with brain fog because it contains silymarin, a potent antioxidant that supports liver health and reduces inflammation. When taken regularly, milk thistle can help clear out toxins and heavy metals that may be contributing to brain fog. Additionally, its ability to promote glutathione production and regeneration can aid in the detoxification of the nervous system, leading to improved cognitive function, increased focus, and a clearer mental state. As a result, individuals taking milk thistle capsules often report feeling more mentally alert and focused throughout the day.
remedy_properties: Silymarin: Milk thistle's main active compound, silymarin, has antioxidant and anti-inflammatory effects that protect the brain from oxidative stress and inflammation, which can contribute to brain fog.

Flavonoids: The flavonoid compounds in milk thistle capsules have been shown to improve cognitive function by increasing blood flow to the brain and protecting against age-related cognitive decline, which can help alleviate symptoms of brain fog.

Saponins: Saponins, another class of compounds found in milk thistle, have been shown to enhance memory and cognitive function by reducing inflammation and oxidative stress in the brain, which can help improve focus and mental clarity.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in flavonoids and silymarin, which helps to protect the liver and promote antioxidant activity to alleviate cognitive impairment associated with brain fog.
Seeds: Containing a unique combination of fatty acids, particularly omega-3 and omega-6, that support brain health and may help reduce inflammation contributing to brain fog.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh silybum marianum leaves and flowers in the morning after dew has dried ensuring maximum potency.
Dry the harvested plant material by spreading it on paper bags for two weeks or using a food dehydrator at 60c.
Crush the dried plant material into smaller pieces using mortar and pestle to release essential oils and resins.
Mix crushed silybum marianum with 50g of powdered vegetable fiber in a bowl and stir well to combine ensuring even distribution.
Fill empty gelatin capsules with a measured amount of the herbal mixture equivalent to 500mg of silybum marianum per capsule.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Chebulic myrobalan capsules helps with brain fog because of its potent antioxidant properties that help to reduce oxidative stress in the brain. This Ayurvedic herb is known to enhance cognitive function, improve memory and concentration, and even boost mood. Its anti-inflammatory properties also help to alleviate feelings of mental fatigue and fogginess. Additionally, chebulic myrobalan has been shown to improve blood flow to the brain, which can help to increase mental clarity and focus. Overall, it's a natural and effective way to combat brain fog and promote overall brain health.
remedy_properties: Antioxidant properties: Chebulic Myrobalan's antioxidants help neutralize free radicals that can damage brain cells and contribute to cognitive impairment, reducing brain fog.
Anti-inflammatory properties: The capsule's anti-inflammatory compounds reduce inflammation in the brain, which is believed to contribute to brain fog, fatigue, and poor memory.

Memory-enhancing properties: Chebulic Myrobalan contains compounds that have been shown to improve memory and cognitive function by increasing blood flow to the brain and protecting against age-related decline.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of Chebulic Myrobalan have been traditionally used to improve cognitive function and memory, making them a prime candidate for creating capsules that alleviate brain fog.
Fruits: The fruits of Chebulic Myrobalan are rich in antioxidants and flavonoids, which can help to enhance mental clarity and reduce oxidative stress associated with brain fog.
remedy_recipe: Harvest high-quality terminalia chebula fruits from reputable sources in the desired quantity.
Dry the fresh terminalia chebula fruits using shade or sunlight for at least two weeks or until they become brittle.
Grind 30 grams of dried terminalia chebula into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle or spice grinder.
Mix the terminalia chebula powder with 100 milliliters of water to create an extract, allowing it to steep for 20 minutes before straining.
Fill empty vegetable capsules with 250 milligrams each of the extracted terminalia chebula mixture, storing them in a cool dry place until consumption.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Licorice capsules helps with brain fog because they contain compounds that have a profound impact on cognitive function. The active ingredients in licorice root, such as glycyrrhizin and isoliquiritigenin, have been shown to improve blood flow to the brain, reducing feelings of mental fogginess and confusion. Additionally, licorice's adaptogenic properties help to reduce stress and anxiety, which are common underlying causes of brain fog. By supporting healthy blood flow and reducing mental tension, herbal licorice capsules can help clarify mental focus and improve overall cognitive performance.
remedy_properties: Glycyrrhizin: This compound has anti-inflammatory properties, which can help reduce inflammation in the brain and alleviate symptoms of brain fog caused by chronic stress or anxiety.

Flavonoids: Licorice root contains flavonoids like luteolin and isoliquiritigenin, which have antioxidant and neuroprotective effects that can help protect against oxidative stress and damage to neural tissues associated with brain fog.

Isomaltose: This sugar molecule has been shown to have a positive effect on memory and cognitive function by increasing the production of neurotransmitters like dopamine and acetylcholine, which are involved in attention, memory, and focus.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in glycyrrhizin, a compound that helps reduce oxidative stress and inflammation in the brain, leading to improved cognitive function.
Barks: Containing bioactive compounds like flavonoids and phenolic acids, which have been shown to enhance memory and cognitive performance.
remedy_recipe: Measure and dry 100g of dried glycyrrhiza glabra roots for 2 hours at 50°c to preserve potency.
Grind the dried root into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle for 5 minutes until smooth.
Mix the powdered root with 500ml of distilled water in a saucepan and simmer for 30 minutes at 90°c.
Strain the liquid through a cheesecloth and filter again to remove any impurities, resulting in 200ml of tincture.
Fill empty capsules with 250mg of the tincture and seal them immediately, ready for consumption in 1-2 weeks.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Five flavor berry capsules helps with brain fog because they are rich in antioxidants, vitamins, and minerals that enhance cognitive function and memory. The blend of herbs, including Bacopa monnieri, Ginkgo biloba, and Schisandra chinensis, work synergistically to improve blood flow to the brain, boosting focus and clarity. Additionally, the capsules contain adaptogenic properties that help the body adapt to stress, reducing mental fatigue and increasing mental acuity. This natural combination promotes a clear and focused mind, helping individuals overcome brain fog and stay mentally sharp.
remedy_properties: Cerebroprotective effects: The combination of herbs in the capsules may help protect the brain from oxidative stress and inflammation, which can contribute to brain fog by promoting healthy neuronal function.
Antioxidant activity: The rich antioxidant content of the herbal blend may help neutralize free radicals that can damage brain cells and contribute to cognitive decline, including brain fog.

Adaptogenic properties: The herbs in the capsules may help adapt to stress and promote balance in the body's physiological response to stress, which can help alleviate symptoms of brain fog such as mental fogginess and decreased focus.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in antioxidants and flavonoids, which help to improve cognitive function and reduce inflammation associated with brain fog.
Rhyzomes: Contain compounds that have been shown to increase blood flow to the brain, improving mental clarity and reducing symptoms of brain fog.
remedy_recipe: Measure out 30 grams of dried schisandra chinensis fruit powder.
Combine the schisandra chinensis fruit powder with 100 milliliters of boiling water.
Allow the mixture to steep for 10 minutes and then filter out any solids.
Mix the liquid extract with 500 milliliters of glycerin and 250 milliliters of distilled water.
Fill empty gelatin capsules with the resulting herbal extract and store them in an airtight container.

supplementary_causes: Ailments similar to brain fog that are treated with herbal capsules are cognitive decline, memory loss, and fatigue. Herbal remedies such as Ginkgo biloba, Bacopa monnieri, and Acetyl-L-carnitine (ALCAR) are commonly used to support mental clarity and focus. Other herbs like St. John's Wort and Rhodiola rosea are also used to alleviate symptoms of depression, anxiety, and stress that can contribute to brain fog. These herbal capsules aim to enhance cognitive function, improve mood, and promote overall well-being.
supplementary_best_treatment: The best combination of herbal capsules that help with brain fog is a blend of Bacopa Monnieri, Ginkgo Biloba, and Acetyl-L-Carnitine (ALCAR). Bacopa Monnieri enhances memory and cognitive function, while Ginkgo Biloba improves blood flow to the brain, promoting oxygenation and clarity. ALCAR, on the other hand, supports the production of energy for brain cells, reducing fatigue and mental fogginess. This synergistic blend helps to boost mental clarity, focus, and concentration, making it an effective natural remedy for brain fog.

remedies.remedy_desc: Bacopa monnieri capsules helps with brain fog because they contain a potent neuroprotective compound that enhances memory and cognitive function. This herb has been traditionally used in Ayurvedic medicine to boost mental clarity and focus. The active ingredients in Bacopa monnieri capsules, such as bacosides, improve neuronal communication and synaptic plasticity, allowing for better recall and learning. By supporting brain health, Bacopa monnieri capsules can help alleviate mental fatigue and brain fog, promoting a clear and focused mind.
remedy_constituents: Bacosides: Bacosides help with brain fog by enhancing cognitive function and memory by protecting neurons from oxidative stress and inflammation.
Bacopaside I: Bacopaside I helps by reducing inflammation and oxidative stress in the brain, which can contribute to cognitive decline and brain fog.
Jasmonic acid: Jasmonic acid helps by promoting the growth of new neurons and improving synaptic plasticity, which can lead to improved memory and cognitive function.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Leaves are the primary source of bacosides, which are responsible for the brain-boosting and cognitive-enhancing properties of Bacopa monnieri.
Stems: Stems of the plant contain bacosides, which help in improving memory and cognitive function.
Roots: Roots of Bacopa monnieri are also used to extract bacosides, contributing to the plant's neuroprotective and memory-enhancing effects.
remedy_recipe: Weigh 500 grams of dried bacopa monnieri root using a digital scale accurately.
Grind 250 grams of the dried root into a fine powder using a spice grinder quickly.
Mix the powder with 100 grams of gum arabic powder and 50 grams of cornstarch powder thoroughly.
Fill empty gelatin capsules with 500 milligrams of the powder mixture using a small spoon carefully.
Store the filled capsules in a cool dark place for six months before consumption.

remedies.remedy_desc: Ginkgo biloba capsules helps with brain fog because they improve blood flow to the brain, increasing oxygen and nutrient delivery to brain cells. This enhanced circulation also helps to reduce inflammation and oxidative stress, which can contribute to cognitive decline. As a result, ginkgo biloba capsules may enhance focus, memory, and mental clarity, reducing feelings of mental fogginess and fatigue. Additionally, the antioxidants present in ginkgo biloba may help to protect brain cells from damage, promoting overall brain health and function.
remedy_constituents: Bilobalide: It helps improve blood flow to the brain by enhancing the function of blood vessels, thus reducing brain fog caused by reduced oxygenation.
Quercetin: It has antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, which can help protect brain cells from damage and promote better cognitive function.

Flavonoids (including kaempferol and isorhapontigenin): These compounds can help improve memory and cognitive function by reducing oxidative stress and inflammation in the brain, and also by modulating neurotransmitter activity.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Leaves are the most widely used part of Ginkgo biloba due to their high concentration of flavonoids and terpenoids, which are believed to improve blood flow to the brain.
Seeds: Seeds are used due to their high content of bilobalide, a compound that is thought to enhance cognitive function and memory.
Barks: Barks are used due to their rich content of flavonoids and terpenoids, which may help improve blood flow to the brain and alleviate symptoms of brain fog.
remedy_recipe: Measure out 250 grams of ginkgo biloba dried leaves and 100 grams of vegetable cellulose powder.
Grind the ginkgo biloba dried leaves into a fine powder using a spice grinder or coffee grinder.
Mix the ground ginkgo biloba powder with the vegetable cellulose powder in a large bowl.
Use a capsule machine to fill empty capsules with the ginkgo biloba mixture 500 mg per capsule.
Label and date the capsules and store them in a cool dark place for up to 6 months.

remedies.remedy_desc: Panax ginseng capsules helps with brain fog because they contain adaptogenic compounds that promote cognitive clarity and focus. Ginsenosides, the active ingredients in Panax ginseng, have been shown to enhance blood flow to the brain, increasing oxygen delivery and nutrient uptake. This improved neural function can help alleviate mental fogginess, fatigue, and lack of concentration. By supporting the brain's natural energy and vitality, Panax ginseng capsules may help individuals regain mental acuity and perform daily tasks with greater ease and efficiency.
remedy_constituents: Ginsenosides: These triterpenoid saponins help with brain fog by improving cognitive function, memory, and concentration due to their neuroprotective and anti-inflammatory properties.
Polyphenols: These compounds help with brain fog by reducing oxidative stress, improving blood flow to the brain, and enhancing the growth of new neurons, which can improve mental clarity and focus.

Terpenoids (specifically, Ginsenol): These compounds help with brain fog by reducing inflammation, modulating neurotransmitters, and improving the integrity of the blood-brain barrier, leading to enhanced cognitive function and reduced brain fatigue.
remedy_parts: Ginsenosides from the Roots: They are the primary bioactive compounds responsible for the medicinal properties of Panax ginseng, including its cognitive-boosting effects.
Ginsenosides from the Rhyzomes: Similar to the roots, the ginsenosides present in the rhyzomes have been found to have neuroprotective and nootropic properties, helping to improve brain function.

Ginsenosides from the Stems: Although less commonly used, the ginsenosides present in the stems of Panax ginseng have been found to have similar cognitive-enhancing effects as those found in the roots and rhyzomes.
remedy_recipe: Dry panax ginseng root in a low-temperature oven at 150°f for 2 hours to preserve its medicinal properties.
Grind 1 gram of dried panax ginseng root into a fine powder using a coffee grinder.
Mix the powder with 1 gram of gelatin in a bowl to create a uniform blend.
Fill gelatin capsules with the panax ginseng powder mixture using a capsule filler machine.
Store the herbal panax ginseng capsules in a cool dry place for up to 2 years.

remedies.remedy_desc: Rhodiola rosea capsules helps with brain fog because they contain adaptogenic properties that allow the body to respond to stress more effectively. This herbal remedy can increase the production of neurotransmitters, such as dopamine and serotonin, which play a crucial role in maintaining focus and mental clarity. Additionally, Rhodiola rosea capsules have been shown to improve mental performance, boost mood, and enhance memory, leading to improved cognitive function and reduced feelings of brain fog. This can result in increased productivity and better overall mental well-being.
remedy_constituents: Rosavin: It helps alleviate brain fog by enhancing memory and cognitive function due to its adaptogenic properties.
Salidroside: This constituent is known to improve mental clarity and reduce fatigue by modulating neurotransmitter activity and promoting antioxidant defenses.
Tyrosol: It assists in reducing brain fog by increasing alertness and mental performance, possibly due to its ability to stimulate the release of neurotransmitters like dopamine and serotonin.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots are the most commonly used part due to their high concentration of active compounds, including rosavin and salidroside, which contribute to the herb's adaptogenic properties.
Rhyzomes: The rhyzomes are used because they contain a higher amount of salidroside, a key compound responsible for the herb's ability to reduce stress and improve mental clarity.

Leaves: The leaves are used as they contain a smaller amount of active compounds, but are still effective in supporting mental health and reducing brain fog when combined with other Rhodiola rosea parts.
remedy_recipe: Crush 500mg of rhodiola rosea roots into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle for 10 minutes.
Mix the powder with 500mg of gelatin in a bowl and stir until well combined for 5 minutes.
Add 50ml of distilled water to the bowl and stir until the gelatin dissolves completely for 2 minutes.
Use a capsule machine to fill the gelatin mixture into empty vegetable capsules for 30 minutes.
Store the filled capsules in an airtight container in a cool dry place for up to 2 years.

remedies.remedy_desc: Centella asiatica capsules helps with brain fog because of its unique ability to improve cognitive function and reduce fatigue. This herbal supplement is rich in triterpenoids, which have potent neuroprotective properties that help to enhance memory, concentration, and mental clarity. By boosting blood flow to the brain and promoting the growth of new neurons, Centella asiatica capsules can help to alleviate the symptoms of brain fog, leading to improved mental performance, focus, and overall well-being. Regular use can also enhance mood and reduce stress levels.
remedy_constituents: Triterpenoids: These compounds help improve cognitive function by enhancing blood flow and oxygenation to the brain, reducing brain fog and improving mental clarity.
Glycosides (specifically Asiaticoside): Asiaticoside has been shown to protect brain cells from damage and promote the growth of new neurons, which can help alleviate brain fog and improve memory.

Flavonoids (specifically Quercetin): Quercetin has anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce oxidative stress and inflammation in the brain, which can contribute to brain fog and cognitive decline.
remedy_parts: Leaves: They are rich in triterpenoids and glycosides, which are believed to have neuroprotective and nootropic effects.
Stems: They contain compounds like madecassoside and asiaticoside, which are thought to improve cognitive function and reduce inflammation.
Roots: They are a source of asiatic acid and other triterpenoids, which may help to enhance memory and focus.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 500g of fresh centella asiatica leaves and stems when they are in full bloom for best potency.
Dry the harvested plant material in a warm place for 7-10 days to reduce moisture content.
Grind the dried plant material into a fine powder using a high-speed grinder for 5 minutes.
Mix 500mg of the powdered plant material with 100mg of gelatin in a small bowl.
Fill 100 capsules with the gelatin mixture and seal them immediately to preserve potency and freshness.

remedies.remedy_desc: Withania somnifera capsules helps with brain fog because they contain adaptogenic properties that promote mental clarity and focus. The herb, also known as ashwagandha, has been used in Ayurvedic medicine for centuries to enhance cognitive function and reduce stress-induced mental fogginess. By regulating cortisol levels and increasing the production of neurotransmitters, ashwagandha supports a healthy brain function, allowing individuals to stay focused and mentally sharp throughout the day. This leads to improved productivity and overall mental well-being.
remedy_constituents: Withanolides: These compounds help with brain fog by reducing inflammation and oxidative stress in the brain, promoting neural health and cognitive function.
Alkaloids (Withanone): These constituents help alleviate brain fog by enhancing memory, improving focus, and reducing fatigue, possibly through their neuroprotective and adaptogenic properties.

Saponins (Withanoside): These compounds help combat brain fog by promoting neuronal health, reducing stress and anxiety, and improving cognitive processing speed, possibly through their antioxidant and anti-inflammatory effects.
remedy_parts: Roots: Roots: The part of the plant that is rich in bioactive compounds, including withanolides, which are responsible for its cognitive-boosting properties.
Leaves: Leaves: The leaves of Withania somnifera are used to enhance cognitive function, as they contain alkaloids and withanolides that promote neural activity.
Seeds: Seeds: Withania somnifera seeds are also used to support brain health, as they contain withanolides and other compounds that may help alleviate brain fog and improve mental clarity.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 500 grams of withania somnifera roots in the morning after the dew has dried.
Dry the harvested roots in a single layer at 50-60 degrees celsius for 3 days.
Grind the dried roots into a fine powder using a coffee grinder or mortar and pestle.
Mix 250 grams of the powder with 250 grams of gelatin or vegetable capsules in a bowl.
Fill 200-250 capsules with the powder-gelatin mixture using a capsule filler machine and seal.

remedies.remedy_desc: Schisandra chinensis capsules helps with brain fog because they are rich in antioxidants and adaptogenic compounds that promote mental clarity and focus. The capsules contain the fruit of the Schisandra chinensis plant, which has been traditionally used in Chinese medicine to enhance cognitive function and memory. By supporting the body's natural response to stress, Schisandra chinensis capsules may help to reduce mental fatigue and improve concentration, leading to a clearer and more focused mind. This can be particularly beneficial for individuals experiencing brain fog due to stress or fatigue.
remedy_constituents: Schisandrins: These lignans have neuroprotective properties, which may help improve cognitive function and reduce brain fog by protecting against oxidative stress and inflammation.
Schisandrin B: This lignan has antioxidant properties, which may help reduce oxidative stress and inflammation in the brain, thereby improving focus and mental clarity.
Gomisin A: This lignan has anti-inflammatory properties, which may help reduce inflammation in the brain and improve cognitive function, including reducing brain fog and improving memory.
remedy_parts: Fruits: Used to make capsules for brain fog due to their rich content of schisandrins, which have antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties.
Barks: Used to make capsules for brain fog due to their high content of schisandrins, which help improve cognitive function and memory.
Leaves: Used to make capsules for brain fog due to their antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, which help reduce oxidative stress and inflammation in the brain.
remedy_recipe: Harvest schisandra chinensis fruits in late summer to early fall when they are fully ripe and dry.
Clean and dry the schisandra chinensis fruits in a low temperature oven for 2 hours at 150 degrees fahrenheit.
Grind 30-60 grams of schisandra chinensis fruits into a fine powder using a coffee grinder.
Mix the powder with 500-600 mg of gelatin in a ratio of 1:1 and add 10-20 ml of water.
Fill 0-00 sized gelatin capsules with the mixture using a capsule filler and store in an airtight container.

remedies.remedy_desc: Vitex agnus-castus capsules helps with brain fog because it supports hormonal balance, particularly estrogen levels, which play a crucial role in cognitive function. By regulating estrogen, Vitex agnus-castus capsules may help alleviate symptoms of hormonal imbalance, such as brain fog, fatigue, and memory issues. Additionally, this herb has been shown to have a positive effect on neurotransmitters, including dopamine and serotonin, which are essential for maintaining focus and mental clarity. This results in improved mental acuity and reduced brain fog.
remedy_constituents: Iridoids: Iridoids help with brain fog by improving memory and cognitive function through their neuroprotective and antioxidant properties, which counteract oxidative stress and inflammation in the brain.
Flavonoids: Flavonoids help with brain fog by enhancing blood flow to the brain and improving neuronal communication, which can help alleviate symptoms of cognitive impairment and fatigue.
Neolignans: Neolignans help with brain fog by inhibiting the production of cortisol, a hormone associated with stress, which can contribute to brain fog and impaired cognitive function.
remedy_parts: Flowers: The flowers are used to make capsules for brain fog due to their high concentration of agnuside, a bioactive compound that may help alleviate symptoms of brain fog.
Leaves: The leaves are used to make capsules for brain fog because they contain a variety of polyphenols and flavonoids, which may have neuroprotective properties.
Seeds: The seeds are used to make capsules for brain fog due to their high content of phytoestrogens, which may help regulate hormonal imbalances contributing to brain fog.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 20-30 dried vitex agnus-castus berries at the end of the flowering season for maximum potency.
Grind 100g of the dried berries into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle for 5 minutes.
Mix 50g of the powder with 50g of a carrier such as tapioca starch to prevent clumping.
Fill empty gelatin capsules with 200-300mg of the powder mixture using a powder funnel for accuracy.
Store the filled capsules in an airtight container in a cool dark place to preserve potency.

remedies.remedy_desc: Ocimum sanctum capsules helps with brain fog because they contain the essence of holy basil, a herb known for its cognitive-boosting properties. The capsules support brain health by reducing oxidative stress and inflammation, which can impede mental clarity. They also promote the production of neurotransmitters such as dopamine and serotonin, enhancing focus and concentration. Furthermore, Ocimum sanctum capsules have been shown to improve memory and mental processing speed, making it easier to stay focused and productive throughout the day.
remedy_constituents: Urosides: Urosides help with brain fog by enhancing cognitive function and memory due to their antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, which protect the brain from oxidative stress and inflammation.
Carvacrol: Carvacrol helps with brain fog by improving mental clarity and focus through its action on the brain's neurotransmitters, which can help alleviate symptoms of cognitive impairment.
Apigenin: Apigenin helps with brain fog by modulating the activity of neurotransmitters and reducing oxidative stress, leading to improved cognitive function and reduced fatigue.
remedy_parts: Leaves: They are rich in essential oils, particularly eugenol and linalool, which have anti-inflammatory and neuroprotective properties that help alleviate brain fog.
Stems: The stems contain bioactive compounds like eugenol and ocimumoside A, which have neuroprotective and antioxidant effects that can aid in brain fog relief.
Leaves: They are also a rich source of antioxidants, including flavonoids and phenolic acids, which help protect the brain from oxidative stress and inflammation associated with brain fog.
remedy_recipe: Dry 500g of ocimum sanctum leaves in a low-temperature oven at 50c for 2 hours to remove moisture content.
Grind the dried leaves into fine powder using a spice grinder at 1000 rpm for 2 minutes.
Mix 10g of the ocimum sanctum powder with 10g of starch and 5g of gelatin in a bowl.
Fill 100 gelatin capsules with the prepared mixture using an automatic capsule filling machine at 10 capsules per minute.
Store the filled capsules in an airtight container in a cool dark place for up to 6 months.

remedies.remedy_desc: Panax quinquefolius capsules helps with brain fog because they contain a wealth of antioxidants and adaptogenic compounds that support cognitive function. The plant's bioactive molecules, such as ginsenosides, help to improve blood flow to the brain, increase neural activity, and enhance the communication between neurons. By reducing inflammation and promoting a healthy balance of neurotransmitters, Panax quinquefolius capsules can help alleviate mental fogginess, improve focus, and boost overall mental clarity. This leads to increased productivity and a sharper mind.
remedy_constituents: Ginsenosides: These ginsenosides help with brain fog by enhancing cognitive function and memory, possibly due to their ability to modulate neurotransmitters and promote neuronal health.

Amino acid precursors (e.g., arginine, tyrosine): Amino acid precursors found in Panax quinquefolius may help with brain fog by providing the necessary building blocks for neurotransmitter synthesis, thereby supporting improved focus and mental clarity.

Phenolic acids (e.g., ginsenoside-Rg1): Phenolic acids present in Panax quinquefolius may help with brain fog by exhibiting antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, which can help mitigate oxidative stress and inflammation in the brain and promote overall cognitive well-being.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of Panax quinquefolius are used to make capsules for brain fog due to their high content of ginsenosides, which are believed to have neuroprotective and cognitive-enhancing properties.

Stems: The stems of Panax quinquefolius contain a lesser amount of ginsenosides but still contribute to the overall cognitive benefits of the plant, making them a potential ingredient in brain fog supplements.

Leaves: The leaves of Panax quinquefolius are also used, although to a lesser extent, as they contain smaller amounts of ginsenosides and other bioactive compounds that may help alleviate brain fog and improve cognitive function.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 30-40 grams of dried panax quinquefolius roots and clean them thoroughly to remove impurities.
Grind the roots into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle for 10 minutes.
Mix 2 grams of the powder with 10 grams of gelatin and 5 grams of water to create a gel-like solution.
Fill a capsule machine with the gel-like solution and fill 60-80 capsules using a dosage of 0.5 grams per capsule.
Label and store the filled capsules in a cool dry place for up to 2 years.
